summary: Trojanized main entry exfiltrates console output to attacker Telegram bot and Firebase
details: Package '0e' is a clear supply-chain trojan. The published main entry (index.js, referenced by package.json main) replaces the global console with a Proxy that forwards every console.log/error/warn/dir invocation to a hardcoded attacker Telegram bot (api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage with attacker chat IDs) and to iiilll.firebaseio.com via PUT, as documented by and corroborated by a static pattern match hits at index.js:63 and src/6cc.js:31. shows the legitimate fetch-wrapper library sits unused in src/ and dist/ while the root shim — which exports no functionality — is wired in as the entrypoint, confirming deliberate trojanization rather than test/demo leftover. Console.error exfiltration will leak stack traces (file paths, secrets embedded in errors) from any consumer. Package name '0e' also fits the short/typosquat profile. Three independent block signals: hardcoded attacker C2 (Telegram+Firebase), global-console hijack on import unrelated to stated purpose, and main-vs-source divergence hiding the payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
